title: Freeze the feasibility-study contract before rerunning
description: Rewrite Stage 2 as a predeclared feasibility and negative-result pilot on the provided CPU-only machine.

steps.step1: Record that LLVM 18.1.3 on this machine emits no usable `-stats -stats-json` counters, so the preregistered `StatsGP` baseline is infeasible and must be skipped rather than replaced.
steps.step2: Record that real double annotation is unavailable in this single-operator run, so the normalization kappa gate remains unsatisfied and any interpretability claim stays gated off.
steps.step3: Retain the six-pass vocabulary, maximum sequence length 4, the fixed warm-up prelude, seeds `{11,17,23}`, and the 18-evaluation budget.
steps.step4: Treat LLVM IR instruction count as descriptive only because it is flat across the full search space on this machine.
steps.step5: Use final binary text size as the executable endpoint, but define the main comparative metric as oracle-gap sample efficiency: evaluations needed to reach the exhaustively measured best text size on non-flat benchmarks.
steps.step6: Limit all claims to a CPU-only feasibility pilot in the restricted six-pass LLVM micro-search space.

title: Freeze the runtime environment
description: Keep the machine contract unchanged and export reproducibility metadata.

steps.step1: Record exact versions of `clang`, `opt`, `llvm-dis`, `llc`, `llvm-size`, and `/usr/bin/time` in `artifacts/env/tool_versions.txt`.
steps.step2: Export the resolved Python package list to `artifacts/env/python_lock.txt`.

title: Assemble the benchmark suite
description: Reuse the screened eight-program benchmark set and the frozen runtime subset from Stage 1.

steps.step1: Canonicalize the fixed benchmark suite to `.bc` and `.ll` under `artifacts/ir/{benchmark}/`.
steps.step2: Write `artifacts/data/benchmark_manifest.csv` with benchmark metadata, canonical IR counts, and screening time.
steps.step3: Write deterministic build and run commands to `artifacts/data/benchmark_commands.yaml` and `artifacts/data/runtime_inputs.yaml`.

title: Run the evidence precondition study
description: Measure remark coverage and verify that the stats path is blocked on this LLVM build.

steps.step1: For each benchmark and each searched pass, run the warm-up prelude plus the single pass with `-pass-remarks*` and `-stats -stats-json` enabled.
steps.step2: Parse raw remarks, normalize them, and write `artifacts/precondition/pass_reliability.csv` and `artifacts/precondition/stats_counter_vocab.json`.
steps.step3: Mark `StatsGP` infeasible if no usable stats counters are emitted and carry that decision forward explicitly.

title: Run the normalization audit
description: Keep the parser-quality measurements but treat kappa as an unmet gate rather than a completed validation.

steps.step1: Build the 120-remark pilot pool, save worked schema examples, and run the rule-based parser on the full pool.
steps.step2: Report parser coverage, `other` rate, unresolved cases, and per-pass failure counts in `artifacts/normalization/pilot_metrics.json`.
steps.step3: Write `SKIPPED.md` and `manual_labels.csv` notes stating that real double annotation and Cohen's kappa remain unavailable in this rerun.

title: Compute fixed and search baselines
description: Run the executable baselines fairly under the revised contract.

steps.step1: Measure standard `default<Oz>` and `warmup+default<Oz>` and duplicate them across seed labels `{11,17,23}`.
steps.step2: Run `Random` with 18 distinct uniformly sampled candidates per benchmark and seed.
steps.step4: Document `StatsGP` as skipped because the environment does not emit usable stats counters.

title: Run the remark-state search baseline and ablations
description: Evaluate the proposed remark-state rule against the executable baselines and its own components.

steps.step1: Run `RemarkState` with the fixed 18-evaluation schedule on all benchmarks and all three seeds.
steps.step2: Run `NoBlocker`, `NoAppliedHistory`, `NoPairProbe`, and beam-width sensitivity (`2`, `3`, `4`) on the fixed runtime subset.
steps.step3: Save all search traces and final sequences to `artifacts/results/search_traces.csv`, `artifacts/results/final_sequences.csv`, and `artifacts/results/ablation_analysis.csv`.

title: Evaluate descriptive outcomes and oracle-gap sample efficiency
description: Use the full enumerated search space as an oracle so the pilot remains informative even if final outcomes tie.

steps.step1: Enumerate the full 1,554-sequence search space per benchmark and record the best reachable text size in `artifacts/results/objective_landscape.csv` and `artifacts/results/objective_landscape_summary.csv`.
steps.step2: Report descriptive final outcomes: instruction-count change, text-size change, runtime speedup on the fixed runtime subset, compile time, and tuning time.
steps.step3: Report main comparative outcomes on non-flat benchmarks: best-so-far oracle gap per evaluation and `first_hit_oracle_eval` for each benchmark, method, and seed.
steps.step4: Write `artifacts/results/success_criteria.json` and `artifacts/results/conclusion_rule.txt` so the rerun is explicitly labeled as a feasibility pilot when blocked prerequisites remain or final outcomes collapse.

title: Generate tables and figures
description: Produce the required artifacts with the revised feasibility framing.

steps.step1: Write benchmark, precondition, main-result, and ablation tables under `tables/`.
steps.step2: Save `figures/precondition_heatmap.*`, `figures/main_comparison_bars.*`, `figures/real_endpoints.*`, and `figures/quality_vs_cost.*` using the revised metrics.
steps.step3: Write the aggregated root `results.json` from the executed artifacts only.
